messages.subject: [R-jobs] R Package developer job at the University of Washington, Seattle
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] R Package developer job at the University of Washington, Seattle
messages.body_plain:
We have a position available for an experienced R programmer to provide support 
for software development for statnet, a suite of R packages for Exponential 
Random Graph Modeling (http://statnet.org -- most content is on the wiki).  The 
current statnet development team includes several PhD level statisticians, some 
faculty from other disciplines, and an independent software developer.  Funding 
is available for at least 2 years, there is some flexibility in hours (75% to 
100% is preferred), and some flexibility in work location.  The official job 
description (and application instructions) are here:

https://uwhires.admin.washington.edu/eng/candidates/default.cfm?szLocationID=88

search for req # 105022

Key skills required include R and C competency; R package development 
experience;  experience with statistical programming (esp. MCMC estimation) and 
network analysis would both be a big plus.  The job will involve a mix of 
software optimization and management of the process for updating the suite of 
statnet packages with regular public releases.

The job description is written to permit a broad range of potential applicants.

Please feel free to forward this email to others who may be interested, and to 
send me an email if you have any questions.

thanks,
Martina Morris

****************************************************************
  Professor of Sociology and Statistics
  Director, UWCFAR Sociobehavioral and Prevention Research Core
  Box 354322
  University of Washington
  Seattle, WA 98195-4322

  Office:        (206) 685-3402
  Dept Office:   (206) 543-5882, 543-7237
  Fax:           (206) 685-7419

morrism at u.washington.edu
http://faculty.washington.edu/morrism/

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Individual contributors and team leaders positions in Nonclinical Statistics at Johnson & Johnson
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Individual contributors and team leaders positions in Nonclinical Statistics at Johnson & Johnson
messages.body_plain:
The Nonclinical Statistics & Computing group at Johnson & Johnson is
searching for individuals to help in the mission of discovering,
developing, manufacturing, and delivering beneficial medicines for
patients. The group resides within the Janssen R&D organization in
Johnson & Johnson's pharmaceutical segment. Realistically we need
individuals with skills and motivation to consistently and adaptively
lead, connect, shape, and deliver. Proficiency with R is a critical
performance factor. There will be endless opportunities to
successfully contribute to the mission and to grow personally.

Links for all of the different positions follow below. Locations in
the United States include Spring House, Pennsylvania, and La Jolla,
California. If you are interested, the links below contain
descriptions and a path to apply. Thank you for your consideration,
and please feel free to forward this to any professionals in your
network you feel may be a good match.

Bill Pikounis
Head, Nonclinical Statistics & Computing at Johnson & Johnson

Senior Biostatistician: http://bit.ly/OeBKZd
Principal Biostatistician: http://bit.ly/OeAKV0
Biostatistician:  http://bit.ly/OeBlpJ
Director: http://bit.ly/OeCtJK
